channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Democrats rattled by postelection changes
item.description: The Democratic Party is grappling with the aftermath of its blowout loss in the presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Woman in coma after trying to save pro golfer's 6-year-old son from drowning
item.description: Professional golfer Brian Harman revealed that a close family friend has been in a coma for weeks after trying to save his 6-year-old son from a near-drowning.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, KRON4

item.title: ICE arrests alleged child sex predator, MS-13 gang member in Massachusetts
item.description: ICE officers operating in Massachusetts have arrested an alleged child sexual predator and MS-13 gang member in the last two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CDC confirms bird flu in a child in California, first reported avian influenza virus in child
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said on Friday it has confirmed a human infection with H5N1 bird flu in a child in California. This is the first reported avian influenza H5 virus infection in a child in the nation.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Man gets probation for helping Young Dolph's killers after Memphis rapper's shooting
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man who helped two hired killers after they gunned down rapper Young Dolph at a Memphis bakery will not serve time in prison under a sentence of six years' probation, a judge ruled Friday.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: New York judge calls off Trump hush money sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump’s sentencing in his New York criminal case will not go forward as planned next week as his attorneys push to dismiss the prosecution following his election victory. Judge Juan Merchan, who oversees the trial proceedings, announced the adjournment in a letter Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal shifts spotlight to Hegseth, Gabbard, Kennedy
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz's (R-Fla.) decision to withdraw from consideration to become attorney general has shifted the attention of Senate Republicans to the nominations of Pete Hegseth, former Hawaii Rep. Tulsi Gabbard and Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9

item.title: Elderly man accused of roommate and dog's 'brutal' murder had extensive criminal record
item.description: Austin police said an elderly man has been arrested for the "very brutal" deadly attack on his roommate and her dog, then using her credit cards and planning to flee the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Your paycheck may be larger in 2025, even if you don't get a raise
item.description: While there's plenty of time left in November, you may already have something exciting to look forward to in 2025: a slightly larger paycheck, even without a raise.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Colorado funeral home owners who let nearly 190 bodies decay plead guilty to corpse abuse
item.description: Colorado funeral home owners accused of letting nearly 190 bodies decay in a room-temperature building and giving grieving families fake ashes have pleaded guilty to corpse abuse.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about a Wisconsin man who faked his own death and fled to Eastern Europe
item.description: A sheriff says a Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning this summer and abandoned his wife and three children is in Eastern Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump has promised again to release the last JFK files. But experts say don’t expect big revelations
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — More than 60 years after President John F. Kennedy was assassinated, conspiracy theories still swirl and any new glimpse into the fateful day of Nov. 22, 1963, in Dallas continues to fascinate.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pathologist disputes finding that Marine veteran's chokehold caused subway rider's death
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For roughly six minutes, Jordan Neely was pinned to a subway floor in a chokehold that ended with him lying still. But that's not what killed him, a forensic pathologist testified Thursday in defense of the military-trained commuter charged with killing Neely. Dr.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC stabbing spree shows failures of criminal justice system: Mayor
item.description: “It’s a public health safety issue,” Adams said regarding the triple homicide at a news conference Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Passengers duct tape unruly man who tried to open door on American Airlines plane
item.description: A group of passengers aboard a Dallas-bound flight restrained a man with duct tape after he claimed to be the "captain" and attempted to open the cabin door of an American Airlines plane midair, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KRON4

item.title: What to know about a storm bringing high winds, heavy rain, snow to California and Pacific Northwest
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — One of the strongest storms on the West Coast in decades knocked out power for thousands of people, unleashed strong winds that toppled trees and left two dead in Washington before making its way through Oregon to Northern California where on Thursday it dropped heavy snow and r
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Number of women who are state lawmakers inches up to a record high
item.description: Women will for the first time make up a majority of state legislators in Colorado and New Mexico next year, but at least 13 states saw losses in female representation after the November election, according to a count released Thursday by the Rutgers Center for American Women and Politics.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: OpenTable names 2024's 'Top 100 Restaurants' in America
item.description: OpenTable's analysts based their findings on more than 14 million diner reviews, along with diner ratings, reservation demand, and percentage of five-star reviews, among other factors.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin man who faked his own kayaking death fled the country and won't come home, officials say
item.description: Borgwardt, 45, was reported missing on Aug. 12 after he failed to return home from kayaking on Big Green Lake, the Green Lake County Sheriff’s Office said at the time.
item.dc:creator: Ben Newhouse, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gaetz withdraws as Trump's pick for attorney general
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) -- Matt Gaetz withdrew Thursday as President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general following continued scrutiny over a federal sex trafficking investigation that cast doubt on his ability to be confirmed as the nation's chief federal law enforcement officer.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Illinois Supreme Court reverses actor Jussie Smollett's conviction
item.description: The Illinois Supreme Court has reversed the conviction of actor Jussie Smollett, who was found guilty of falsely reporting a 2019 hate crime, after he argued that the State's second prosecution was a due process violation.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Attack of the trees: Aging giants wreak havoc in Missouri neighborhood
item.description: "We’re pretty concerned. We’re thinking, ‘Should we move before the trees destroy our home?’” said one resident.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, Fox 5

item.title: US charges one of the world's richest people with fraud
item.description: Indian businessman Gautam Adani, one of the world's richest people, was charged by the U.S. on allegations of fraud.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Assault allegations against Hegseth revealed in police report
item.description: A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sing-along screenings of 'Wicked' coming soon as AMC issues strict warning to fans
item.description: Fans eager to belt out hits like "Defying Gravity" and "Popular" in theaters during the highly anticipated "Wicked" movie musical will need to hold off — for now.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, ABC 4

item.title: CMA Awards: 2024 Winners | LIST
item.description: Keep up with all the biggest winners and moments from Country Music’s Biggest Night™ at the 58th Annual CMA Awards live from Nashville, TN!
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Posey, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pamela Hayden, longtime 'Simpsons' voice actor, hangs up her mic
item.description: “It’s been an honor and a joy to have worked on such a funny, witty, and groundbreaking show,” Hayden said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Man wins $650K on lottery ticket using numbers tied to late wife
item.description: A Chicago man won a $650,000 Lucky Day Lotto jackpot prize after playing numbers related to his late wife.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Texas offers Trump land on US-Mexico border for potential mass deportations
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Texas is offering a parcel of rural ranchland along the U.S.-Mexico border to use as a staging area for potential mass deportations under President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy lay out plans for 'mass' federal layoffs, rule rollbacks under Trump
item.description: Tech entrepreneurs Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy said Wednesday that their brand-new government efficiency panel will identify “thousands” of regulations for President-elect Donald Trump to eliminate, which they argue will justify “mass head-count reductions” across government.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amid X exodus, Bluesky CEO says 1M people per day joined in past week
item.description: The social media platform Bluesky has emerged as a popular alternative for users departing Elon Musk's X and other platforms, with nearly 1 million people joining each day over the past week, Bluesky CEO Jay Graber said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, ABC 4

item.title: Florida man files lawsuit against Netflix over Tyson-Paul fight streaming issues
item.description: A class action lawsuit was filed against Netflix over alleged streaming issues during the fight between legendary brawler Mike Tyson and former YouTuber Jake Paul.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, ABC 4

item.title: Susan Smith denied parole 30 years after drowning sons by rolling car into South Carolina lake
item.description: Susan Smith made national headlines in 1994 after she rolled her car into a lake with both of her children strapped in their seats.
item.dc:creator: Dustin George, ABC 4

item.title: Delta to begin serving Shake Shack on select flights
item.description: Travelers soon will be able to enjoy Shake Shack's famous burgers on select Delta flights beginning next month, with plans to expand throughout the U.S. in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Man convicted of killing nursing student Laken Riley sentenced to life in prison without parole
item.description: The Venezuelan man convicted of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley has been sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Best apps to save and organize your recipes
item.description: How many times have you found a great recipe online only to wonder, "Now, where do I save this?" TikTok and Instagram have built-in bookmark functionality, but these apps not only help you save and organize your collection but also cut through the clutter. Mela, Free to $5, iOS and Mac only.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5
